reasoning: The unsafe verdict was based on a false positive in ensure-mac-dependency.js, which is macOS-specific and inert on Linux. The script is part of upstream code, not malicious, and doesn't execute during Arch builds.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged ensure-mac-dependency.js as unsafe due to runtime npm installs, while deepseek correctly recognized it's inert on Linux and part of normal upstream behavior.
coverage_issues: Neither model reviewed critical install hooks or .install scripts
Network/filesystem access points in src/franz/src/api/server/ServerApi.js weren't deeply analyzed
false_positives: qwen's unsafe verdict on ensure-mac-dependency.js: macOS-specific script that does nothing on Linux, part of upstream code
false_negatives: Both missed potential analysis of network calls in ServerApi.js during actual app runtime
learnings: Prioritize OS context: Models should emphasize target OS (Linux) when evaluating platform-specific code
Improve build/runtime distinction: Clarify prompt to differentiate build-time helpers vs installed application code
Focus file selection: Weight PKGBUILD, install hooks, and code with network/filesystem access higher than UI modules
